content:
The kiln is humming in the back room of the community center, a low, electric thrum that vibrates through the soles of my shoes. It is the only thing in this room that insists on a definite temperature. Outside, the Noriega fog is pressing against the glass, blurring the streetlights into soft, yellow bruises.

I am standing at the sink, washing the grey slip from my forearms. The water is cold—no
